name: Cheesy Stuffed Chicken Breasts
recipeIngredient: 4 Skinless, boneless chicken breast halves (about 1 1/2 pounds)
1 1/2 cups Coarsely shredded Monterey Jack cheese
recipeInstructions:
1. Place each chicken breast half between waxed paper and pound very thin with a mallet or rolling pin to form a "cutlet". Be careful not to create holes in the meat.
2. Place 1/3 cup cheese in the center of each cutlet; sprinkle with oregano. Fold the sides over to seal the cheese, then roll up. Secure with a wooden toothpick.
3. Combine cornmeal with chili powder on waxed paper and set aside. Place flour on another sheet of waxed paper.
4. Beat eggs slightly in a pie plate.
5. Coat each stuffed roll with flour, then dip into egg and finally the cornmeal mixture to coat well. Chill for at least an hour. (This step can be done ahead of time.)
6. Preheat oven to 350°F.
7. In a large skillet, heat oil until very hot. Quickly brown the stuffed chicken rolls, turning often, until golden.
8. Remove with a slotted spoon and drain well. Place in a shallow baking pan.
9. Bake for 20 minutes or until juices run clear when pierced with a small knife.
10. Serve with guacamole and/or salsa.
Makes 4 servings.
